cards.back: A principle of software design that suggests breaking down a system into smaller, independent components, each with a specific responsibility, to improve maintainability, scalability, and reusability.
A principle that states a class, module, or function should have only one reason to change, to improve maintainability and reduce coupling.
A principle that advises against duplicating code or data, to reduce errors, improve maintainability, and make the code more flexible.
A principle that recommends simplicity and minimalism in software design, to reduce complexity, improve readability, and make the system more maintainable.
A principle that advises against adding features or functionality that are not currently needed, to reduce complexity, improve maintainability, and make the system more flexible.
